instruments: Payment Services Act (資金決済に関する法律, Act No. 59 of 2009), Art. 2(15) item 4 -- defines 'management of crypto assets' (他人のために暗号資産の管理をすること) as part of crypto-asset exchange business requiring registration even where the operator runs no exchange business at all; Art. 63-11 (利用者財産の管理, Management of Users' Property) -- segregated management of users' money (via trust) and users' crypto-assets, with periodic CPA/audit-firm audit; Art. 63-11-2 (履行保証暗号資産, Performance-Guarantee Crypto Assets) -- confirmed by direct fetch, e-Gov Law API, law_id 421AC0000000059

Act No. 28 of 2019 (令和元年法律第28号) -- the post-Coincheck PSA/FIEA amendment that brought custody-only business inside the PSA registration perimeter and moved crypto-asset derivatives dealing into the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEA); in force 1 May 2020 by Cabinet Order No. 141 of 3 April 2020

Cabinet Office Ordinance on Crypto-Asset Exchange Service Providers (暗号資産交換業者に関する内閣府令, Cabinet Office Ordinance No. 7 of 2017), Art. 27(2) -- caps the portion of user crypto-assets an operator may hold outside cold-wallet-or-equivalent methods at '百分の五' (5%) of the JPY-equivalent value of crypto-assets it manages for users; Art. 1(2)(7) -- defines 履行保証暗号資産 by direct cross-reference to PSA Art. 63-11-2(1); confirmed by direct fetch, e-Gov Law API, law_id 429M60000002007

Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (金融商品取引法, Act No. 25 of 1948), Art. 2(1) item 3-2 (三の二) -- defines '暗号等資産' (crypto-etc-assets, by cross-reference to PSA Art. 2(14)) as an eligible underlying for market and over-the-counter derivative transactions, the provision that brings crypto-asset derivatives dealing within FIEA's existing Type I financial instruments business licensing framework since the 2019 amendment (1 May 2020); confirmed by direct fetch, e-Gov Law API, law_id 323AC0000000025

Cabinet Office Order on Financial Instruments Business, etc. (金融商品取引業等に関する内閣府令, Cabinet Office Order No. 52 of 2007), Art. 117(1) items 47-50 together with paras. 41-42 -- prohibits continuing a crypto-asset-related derivatives contract (暗号資産等関連デリバティブ取引) without requiring the client to cover a shortfall against the required deposit amount, which paras. 41-42 set at '百分の五十' (50%) of the transaction's notional value -- an effective 2x maximum leverage; confirmed by direct fetch, e-Gov Law API, law_id 419M60000002052

2025 PSA amendment (資金決済に関する法律の一部を改正する法律, 令和7年法律第66号), in force 1 June 2026 -- inserted new PSA Chapter 3-4 (第三章の四 電子決済手段・暗号資産サービス仲介業), PSA Art. 2(18)-(21) definitions and Art. 63-22-2 registration -- a standalone, lighter registration tier for crypto-asset intermediation without the intermediary itself holding user assets; confirmed present in the current consolidated PSA text, direct fetch, e-Gov Law API, law_id 421AC0000000059

Bill for Partial Amendment of the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act and the Payment Services Act (金融商品取引法及び資金決済に関する法律の一部を改正する法律案), submitted to the 221st ordinary Diet session 10 April 2026, passed the House of Representatives 11 June 2026, pending in the House of Councillors' Financial Affairs Committee (referred 15 June 2026) as of this card's generation date (12 July 2026) -- would newly subject crypto-asset investment-management and investment-advisory activity to FIEA's investment management business / investment advisory business regulation, fold crypto-asset trading intermediation into financial instruments intermediary business, and create a notification-based regime for crypto-asset custody system providers; not yet law, cited here as pending legislation only

JVCEA's Rules on the Management of User Assets for crypto-asset exchange business (暗号資産交換業に係る利用者財産の管理等に関する規則) -- amended alongside JVCEA's new/amended System Risk Management, Emergency Response, and Information Security Management rules and guidelines after JVCEA identified deficiencies in it during that same rule-making process; enacted/partially amended 29 June 2026, in force 1 July 2026, per JVCEA's own results notice (jvcea.or.jp/information/pc/20260701-002/), fetched directly this session
standing_summary: Japan regulates dealing, custody and investment-advisory activity in crypto assets not through a single dedicated statute for this pillar but by amendment to the same two existing laws that anchor the rest of the Japanese crypto regime -- the Payment Services Act (PSA) for custody and the newly created lighter-tier intermediary business, and the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEA) for derivatives dealing and, prospectively, spot investment advisory activity -- the same amendment-based structural pattern documented across this jurisdiction's other pillars, not a standalone crypto-custody or crypto-advisory statute. Custody: the 2019 PSA amendment (Act No. 28 of 2019, 令和元年法律第28号, in force 1 May 2020 by Cabinet Order No. 141 of 3 April 2020) extended crypto-asset exchange business registration under PSA Art. 2(15) item 4 to cover '他人のために暗号資産の管理をすること' (managing crypto-assets on behalf of others) even where the operator runs no exchange business at all -- confirmed by direct fetch of the PSA's consolidated text via the e-Gov Law API (law_id 421AC0000000059). Segregation is set out in PSA Art. 63-11 ('利用者財産の管理', Management of Users' Property), which requires a crypto-asset exchange service provider to hold users' money separated from its own money and entrust it to a trust company per Cabinet Office Ordinance, and to manage users' crypto-assets '自己の暗号資産と分別して管理' (segregated from its own crypto-assets), with a periodic certified-public-accountant or audit-firm audit of that segregated-management status under Art. 63-11(3). Article 63-11(2) permits a Cabinet-Ordinance-defined operational exception -- in practice the hot-wallet portion -- and Art. 63-11-2 ('履行保証暗号資産', Performance-Guarantee Crypto Assets) requires the operator to hold, as its own assets, crypto-assets of the same type and quantity as that excepted portion, itself segregated from the operator's other own crypto-assets and separately audited. This pillar's brief flagged the exact hot-wallet ceiling and its legal source (Cabinet Office Ordinance vs. JVCEA self-regulatory rule) as needing live verification; direct fetch of the Cabinet Office Ordinance on Crypto-Asset Exchange Service Providers (暗号資産交換業者に関する内閣府令, Cabinet Office Ordinance No. 7 of 2017, law_id 429M60000002007) confirms the answer is the ordinance, not JVCEA rule: Art. 27(2) caps the portion of user crypto-assets an operator may hold outside 'cold wallet or equivalent' methods at an amount whose JPY-equivalent value does not exceed '百分の五' -- 5% -- of the JPY-equivalent value of the crypto-assets it manages for users, and Art. 1(2)(7) defines 履行保証暗号資産 by direct cross-reference to PSA Art. 63-11-2(1) rather than restating it. Crypto-asset derivatives dealing: the same 2019 PSA amendment moved crypto-asset derivatives dealing out of the PSA and into FIEA. Direct fetch of FIEA's consolidated text via the e-Gov Law API (law_id 323AC0000000025) confirms FIEA Art. 2(1) item 3-2 ('三の二') defines '暗号等資産' (crypto-etc-assets, by cross-reference to PSA Art. 2(14)) as an eligible underlying for market and over-the-counter derivative transactions alongside securities, deposit claims, currency and commodities -- the amendment that brought crypto-asset derivatives dealing within FIEA's existing Type I financial instruments business licensing framework rather than creating a new licence category. This pillar's brief also flagged the leverage cap for live verification rather than assuming 2x; direct fetch of the Cabinet Office Order on Financial Instruments Business, etc. (金融商品取引業等に関する内閣府令, Cabinet Office Order No. 52 of 2007, law_id 419M60000002052) confirms it at the source: Art. 117(1) items 47-50 (禁止行為, prohibited acts) make it a prohibited act for a financial instruments business operator to continue a crypto-asset-related derivatives contract (暗号資産等関連デリバティブ取引) without requiring the client to make up any shortfall against the required deposit amount, and paras. 41 and 42 define that required deposit amount as the transaction's notional value multiplied by '百分の五十' -- 50% -- meaning the operative rule is a 50%-of-notional margin requirement, i.e. an effective 2x maximum leverage, resolving as primary-source-verified what this jurisdiction's Exchanges (VATP) pillar had flagged as corroborated only by secondary legal commentary. New intermediary tier: the 2025 PSA amendment (資金決済に関する法律の一部を改正する法律, 令和7年法律第66号, in force 1 June 2026 per FSA's own 22 May 2026 cabinet-order announcement) inserted a new PSA Chapter 3-4 ('第三章の四 電子決済手段・暗号資産サービス仲介業'), confirmed present in the current consolidated PSA text fetched this session: PSA Art. 2(18) defines '暗号資産仲介行為' as a non-CAESP, acting on entrustment ('委託を受けて') from a registered crypto-asset exchange service provider, mediating ('媒介') the sale/purchase of crypto-assets or their exchange for other crypto-assets on that CAESP's behalf -- intermediation without the intermediary itself holding user assets, which remain with the principal CAESP -- and Art. 63-22-2 requires its own Prime Minister registration, distinct from and lighter than full CAESP registration under Art. 63-2. Investment advisory gap and the pending migration bill: investment advisory business under FIEA today does not extend to advice limited to spot crypto-asset transactions (crypto-asset derivatives advisory is separately covered), a framing corroborated by secondary legal-commentary sources this session but not independently confirmed against FIEA's own advisory-business definition articles in this pass. The pending Bill for Partial Amendment of FIEA and the PSA (金融商品取引法及び資金決済に関する法律の一部を改正する法律案), submitted to the 221st Diet 10 April 2026 and confirmed via direct fetch of FSA's own bill-outline and explanatory-materials PDFs (fsa.go.jp/common/diet/221/02/04.pdf and .../03.pdf) as officially providing, among its crypto-asset provisions, that '暗号資産を投資対象とする投資運用行為や投資助言行為について、それぞれ投資運用業、投資助言業の対象として規制' -- investment-management and investment-advisory conduct targeting crypto-assets as an investment object would each become subject to investment management business and investment advisory business regulation respectively -- would close that gap; the same bill would fold crypto-asset trading intermediation into financial instruments intermediary business ('金融商品仲介業'), and, distinct from the 2025 PSA amendment's intermediary tier described above, create a notification-based (届出, not full registration) regime for providers of systems material to a customer's crypto-asset custody, imposing a duty of care (善管注意義務), business-management-system obligations and supervisory sanctions per the bill's own outline (Art. 2(54)-(55) and Chapter 3-6 cross-references), alongside a new reserve-fund (責任準備金) requirement under amended Art. 46-5 sized to the balance of crypto-assets managed and calibrated to security level, to fund client compensation after an unauthorized-outflow event. Direct fetch of the Diet's own bill-progress pages (shugiin.go.jp for the House of Representatives, sangiin.go.jp for the House of Councillors) confirms the bill passed the House of Representatives 11 June 2026 ('可決') and was transmitted to the House of Councillors the same day, which referred it to its Financial Affairs Committee on 15 June 2026; as of this card's generation date (12 July 2026) no House of Councillors decision has been recorded -- it is not law, and is described here as pending legislation only, per this pillar's brief instruction to re-verify status at run time rather than assume enactment. This pillar covers custody, derivatives dealing and prospective investment-advisory regulation only; JVCEA's self-regulatory rules on listings, new-token sales and advertising, binding on registered exchange operators, are tracked in the Exchanges (VATP) pillar; trust-type stablecoin custody (特定信託受益権) is tracked in the Stablecoins pillar; and AML/CFT duties applicable to custody and dealing intermediaries are tracked in the AML/CFT & Enforcement pillar, to avoid duplicating citations across pillars. On user-asset segregation specifically: JVCEA's own results notice on its 3-15 June 2026 public comment procedure (fetched directly this session) states that, in the course of finalising new/amended System Risk Management, Emergency Response, and Information Security Management rules and guidelines for crypto-asset exchange business, JVCEA identified deficiencies ('不備') in its existing Rules on the Management of User Assets for crypto-asset exchange business (暗号資産交換業に係る利用者財産の管理等に関する規則) and amended that rule alongside the others; all were enacted/partially amended 29 June 2026 and took effect 1 July 2026. JVCEA's notice does not itself describe the specific nature of the deficiencies identified, only that they existed and were corrected.

e-Gov Law API: Cabinet Office Ordinance on Crypto-Asset Exchange Service Providers (暗号資産交換業者に関する内閣府令, Cabinet Office Ordinance No. 7 of 2017) -- full text, JSON (Art. 27(2), Art. 1(2)(7) confirmed live this session)
e-Gov Law API: Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (金融商品取引法, Act No. 25 of 1948) -- full text, JSON (Art. 2(1) item 3-2 confirmed live this session)

e-Gov Law API: Cabinet Office Order on Financial Instruments Business, etc. (金融商品取引業等に関する内閣府令, Cabinet Office Order No. 52 of 2007) -- full text, JSON (Art. 117(1) items 47-50 and paras. 41-42 confirmed live this session)
FSA: crypto-asset business overview page (暗号資産に関連する事業を行うみなさまへ) -- confirms 1 May 2020 registration/notification commencement for custody-only and derivatives businesses
FSA: Bills submitted to the Diet (国会提出法案等) -- lists the pending FIEA/PSA amendment bill and its supporting documents
FSA: Bill overview (法律案の概要), 221st Diet session, official PDF
FSA: Bill explanatory materials (説明資料), 221st Diet session, official PDF -- source of the investment-management/advisory, intermediary and custody-system-provider provisions quoted above
FSA: Bill outline (法律案要綱), 221st Diet session, official PDF
FSA: public comment results on cabinet orders/ministerial ordinances for the 2025 PSA amendment (令和7年法律第66号), including the new intermediary-business ordinance, 22 May 2026
House of Representatives (衆議院): bill progress page, 221st Diet session, bill no. 57 -- confirms House of Representatives passage 11 June 2026
House of Councillors (参議院): bill detail page, 221st Diet session, bill no. 57 -- confirms referral to the Financial Affairs Committee 15 June 2026, no decision recorded as of this session
Japanese Law Translation (Ministry of Justice): Payment Services Act, English courtesy translation -- only the Japanese original has legal effect per the portal's own terms

open_items: This pillar states that investment advisory business under FIEA currently excludes advice limited to spot crypto-asset transactions based on secondary legal-commentary sources (e.g. law-firm bulletins) corroborated this session; it did not independently fetch and quote FIEA's own investment-advisory-business definition articles (Art. 2(8) item 11 and related) to confirm the current scope directly, relying instead on the pending 2026 bill's own stated purpose of newly bringing such advice into FIEA regulation as indirect confirmation of the present gap. Should be re-confirmed against FIEA's own advisory-business definition text before being treated as independently primary-verified.

The pending Bill for Partial Amendment of the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act and the Payment Services Act (submitted 10 April 2026, passed the House of Representatives 11 June 2026) had not been decided by the House of Councillors as of this card's generation date (12 July 2026) -- referred to the House of Councillors' Financial Affairs Committee 15 June 2026, no vote recorded at last check. Its status, and the exact commencement date and transitional mechanics if and when enacted, must be re-verified before being treated as current.

This card's account of the pending bill's new crypto-asset-custody-system-provider notification regime and reserve-fund requirement is drawn from FSA's own bill-outline (要綱) and explanatory-materials PDFs fetched directly this session (fsa.go.jp/common/diet/221/02/03.pdf and 04.pdf); the full draft statutory text (the parallel 01.pdf on the same FSA page) was not independently parsed article-by-article in this pass, so any provision not captured in the outline/explanatory summary should be re-confirmed against the full draft text before being cited as exhaustive.

The '2x maximum leverage' framing for crypto-asset-related derivatives transactions is this card's own arithmetic restatement of the Cabinet Office Order on Financial Instruments Business's own figure -- a required deposit of 50% ('百分の五十') of notional value under Art. 117 paras. 41-42 -- fetched and confirmed directly this session; the ordinance's own text does not use the phrase '2倍' in the provisions fetched, so the 2x figure should be read as a derived restatement rather than a verbatim statutory quote, though the underlying percentage is primary-source-verified.

This session's fetch of the Japanese Law Translation portal's English courtesy translation of the Payment Services Act (japaneselawtranslation.go.jp/en/laws/view/3078) returned an excerpt using pre-2019-amendment terminology ('Virtual Currency' rather than 'Crypto Assets'); it could not be confirmed in this pass whether this reflects the translation's actual current state or an artifact of partial page retrieval. In either case the portal's own terms state that only the Japanese original has legal effect, so no English-language quote from this source was relied upon for an operative legal claim in this card -- all operative figures and definitions above were instead sourced directly from the Japanese consolidated text via the e-Gov Law API.

This pillar covers custody, crypto-asset derivatives dealing and (prospectively) investment-advisory regulation only. JVCEA's self-regulatory rules on listings, new-token sales and advertising are tracked in the Exchanges (VATP) pillar; trust-type stablecoin custody (特定信託受益権) is tracked in the Stablecoins pillar; and AML/CFT duties (including travel-rule obligations) applicable to custody and dealing intermediaries are tracked in the AML/CFT & Enforcement pillar -- not restated here to avoid duplicating citations across pillars.
